texto: I knew this id who very bravely and bossily came out of the closet when she was only fourteen years old. She told me then that we can't choose who we love. We just love the people we love, no matter what anyone else might want for you.
explicacao: Citação de Madeleine George sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “I knew this id who very bravely and bossily came out of the closet when she was only fourteen yea…” articula coragem, lgbt, love em registro de coragem, com relevância para contextos como love.

texto: Anyway, if you need your heroes to be perfect, you won't have very many. Even Superman had his Kryptonite. I'd rather have my heroes be more like me: trying to do the right thing, sometimes messing up. Making mistakes. Saying you're sorry. And forgiving other people when they mess up, too.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Madeleine George: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Anyway, if you need your heroes to be perfect, you won't have very many. Even Superman had his Kr…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, heroes, lessons pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de lessons.

frase_original: Anyway, if you need your heroes to be perfect, you won't have very many. Even Superman had his Kryptonite. I'd rather have my heroes be more like me: trying to do the right thing, sometimes messing up. Making mistakes. Saying you're sorry. And forgiving other people when they mess up, too.

texto: Once," Fran says, settling against the worktable, folding her arms, "I knew this kid who very bravely and bossily came out of the closet when she was only fourteen years old. She told me then that we can't choose who we love. We just love the people we love, no mattter what anyone else might want for us. Wasn't that you?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Madeleine George que provoca vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. Ao questionar “Once," Fran says, settling against the worktable, folding her arms, "I knew this kid who very bra…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre coragem, bravery, coming-out, com foco em trabalho e coming-out.
